slides.notes: Opening. TEKKAL is a local-first slide platform. The stat card previews the whole deck: two entry points — chat in-app or code-edit on disk — over one JSON file format. Everything else is detail.

elements.content: TEKKAL · PRODUCT DECK
# TEKKAL
A Local-First, AI-Agent-Driven, Open-Source Slide Platform
Chat a deck into existence, or hand-edit the files yourself. Your slides live on disk as plain JSON — yours to own, share, and version.

ENTRY POINTS
chat in the app · code-edit on disk
WHAT IT IS
**Local-first** — decks are files, not rows in a database.
**Agent-native** — the same JSON an LLM can call, a human can edit.
**Open-source** — BYOK, self-host, extend, or just read the code.

slides.notes:
The premise — a deck is a plain JSON file on your disk. Left-side subtitle + JSON excerpt auto-reveal.

[step:1]Two-ways teaser opens: (01) coding agents editing deck.json on disk, (02) in-app chat at tekkal.dev. The next two slides go deep on each.[/step]

elements.content: THE IDEA
# Your deck is a file
No database, no proprietary format — just **deck.json** on your disk. Yours to keep, version with git, and edit two different ways.

elements.alt: Photorealistic overhead shot of an architect's desk at night — open notebook with floorplan sketch, brass lamp, fountain pen on deep navy wood

elements.content: TWO WAYS TO EDIT IT
**With a coding agent**
Claude Code · Gemini CLI · Cursor · Antigravity edit deck.json directly on disk.
**In the in-app chat**
Open tekkal.dev, paste a Gemini key, describe what you want, iterate.

slides.notes:
Path A — external coding agents. Because deck.json is a file on disk, any file-aware agent can edit it. Walk the four cards: name, role, example command.

[step:1]Bottom best-for line fades in — bulk refactors, diff review, git workflows.[/step]

elements.alt: Photorealistic aerial panorama of a modern nighttime metropolis from a rooftop

elements.content: PATH A · CODE-EDIT
# Point your coding agent at the file
Your deck is **deck.json** on disk. Any file-aware agent can read, edit, and commit it — the same way it edits source code.
**Claude Code**
Terminal-native. Strong at multi-file edits and git workflows.
$ claude-code "add a summary slide to deck.json"
**Gemini CLI**
One-off shell edits. Quick from inside a project dir.
$ gemini "rename every occurrence of Acme Corp"
**Cursor**
IDE-integrated. Visual diffs, Cmd-K edits, side-by-side review.
$ cursor deck.json   # then ⌘K: "tighten rhythm"
**Antigravity**
Google's agentic IDE. Plans multi-step edits across files.
$ antigravity "apply brand palette to all decks"
**BEST FOR** — bulk refactors · diff review · git workflows · editing many decks at once

slides.notes:
Path B — in-app chat at tekkal.dev. Three steps: paste a Gemini API key, describe the deck you want, iterate. Walk the three numerals aloud.

[step:1]Sample conversation reveals on the right — show how the prompt and the follow-ups evolve the same deck.[/step]

[step:2]Best-for badge fades in at the bottom — fast first drafts, brainstorming, conversational editing.[/step]

elements.alt: Photorealistic warm cafe facade at night with pendant lights glowing behind large windows

elements.content: PATH B · IN-APP CHAT
# Chat a deck into existence
Open tekkal.dev. Paste a Gemini key. Describe what you want. Iterate in natural language.
**Drop in your key**
Open **Settings → API Keys** and paste a Gemini key from Google AI Studio. Stored locally, never sent to us.
**Describe the deck**
Natural language. The planner asks clarifying questions; the generator builds slides, one at a time.
**Iterate by chat**
Refine one slide, restyle the whole deck, or ask for presenter notes. Undo with git, redo with another prompt.
A REAL CONVERSATION

**You** — Make me a 6-slide intro to climate adaptation for a policy audience.

**TEKKAL** — *Drafted 6 slides. Want me to lean more quantitative or narrative?*

**You** — More quantitative. Add a Phillips-curve-style chart on slide 4.

**TEKKAL** — *Swapped slide 4 for a pgfplots figure. Notes updated.*

**You** — Now restyle in Swiss poster colors and tighten the rhythm.
**BEST FOR** — fast first drafts · brainstorming · conversational editing

slides.notes:
Recipes — six concrete prompts audience members can copy into the chat or hand to a coding agent. On enter: tag + title + rule. Each card fades in on a stagger.

[step:1]Unifier line at the bottom — "same prompts work in either path."[/step]

elements.content: RECIPES
# What you can ask for
Same prompts work in either path. Paste them into the in-app chat, or hand them to your coding agent.
CREATE
"Generate a 6-slide pitch for a BYOK research notebook, quantitative tone."
RESTYLE
"Apply a Swiss-poster palette across every slide. Drop the gradient backgrounds."
EXTEND
"Add a QR-code slide linking to github.com/me, then another with speaker bio."
FIX
"Tighten vertical rhythm, fix widows, and normalize heading sizes across the deck."
CONVERT
"Turn the bullets on slide 3 into a TikZ flowchart. Keep the colors."
REVIEW
"Flag any slide where the visual hierarchy is unclear or the notes are too long."
**One schema. Two entrances.** Anything that writes valid JSON is a valid authoring surface.

slides.notes:
Call-to-action — 60-second install. On enter: GET STARTED tag + rule + title + subtitle + bash code auto-reveal.

[step:1]Right panel "WHY BYOK" opens — prompts never touch our servers, decks never leave your disk.[/step]

[step:2]"WHAT YOU GET" — one-line element palette summary. End warm: open source, PRs welcome.

elements.content: GET STARTED
# Start at tekkal.dev
Open the hosted app, paste a Gemini key, and chat your first deck into existence. Prefer local? The source is a `git clone` away.

# primary — hosted web app
open https://tekkal.dev
# 1. paste your Gemini API key into Settings
# 2. type: "make me a 6-slide climate pitch"

# optional — run it locally
git clone https://github.com/tekkal/tekkal.git
cd tekkal && npm install && npm run dev
WHY BYOK
You bring your own API key. Your prompts never touch our servers. Your decks never leave your disk. Your usage costs exactly what your provider charges — nothing more.
WHAT YOU GET
Markdown + KaTeX · TikZ · tables · code · shapes · live 3D · images
